item.title: About Me
item.description: I am Yenson Lau, a machine learning scientist and engineer. Having grown up in Hong Kong and Vancouver, I spent most of my life and career in diverse urban environments. I am currently based in Toronto. Until recently I was at Layer 6 AI, developing models for TD Bank and performing research on graph neural networks for drug repurposing as well as personalized federated learning. Prior to this I investigated optimization methods for deconvolution as a Ph.

item.title: Publications & Preprints
item.description: Amy Sui, Junfeng Wang, Yenson Lau , Brendan Ross, Jesse Cresswell. Find Your Friends: Personalized Federated Learning with the Right Collaborators. In International Workshop on Federated Learning, NeurIPS 2022. Yenson Lau , Saba Zuberi, Jahir Gutierrez. Drug Repurposing for Leishmaniasis Major with Hyperbolic Graph Neural Networks. In submission. Yenson Lau , Qing Qu, Han-Wen Kuo, Pengcheng Zhou, Yuqian Zhang, and John Wright. Short and Sparse Deconvolution — A Geometric Approach.

item.title: Projects
item.description: PII Remediation Evaluation for LLM Pretraining Link to heading The BLOOM large language model (LLM) is the cumulation of BigScience, a one-year collaborative research workshop involving over 1,000 researchers. It was trained in complete transparency, has 176B parameters, and generates text in 46 languages. For most of these languages, BLOOM will be the first language model ever created with over 100B parameters. As LLMs are known to memorize instances of the training data, such undertakings require the careful treatment of Personally Identifiable Information (PII).
